channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Apple’s Emergency SOS Helped Save 6 Skiers After California’s Deadliest Avalanche—Here’s How to Use It Yourself
item.description: It allowed skiers to connect with rescuers via satellite.

item.title: Sam Altman and Dario Amodei doge hand-holding chain during Modi’s AI summit
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday invited leaders of some of the top artificial intelligence companies to gather on stage as part of a c...

item.title: ‘Staples Baddie’ went viral. Here’s what the retailer is doing about its Gen Z employee’s internet fame
item.description: By now, the so-called “Staples Baddie” may have crossed your feed with her tutorials and informational videos exploring her workplace. TikTok creator ...

item.title: Walmart exec says it’s ‘unfortunate’ that other companies are slashing workforces in the name of AI—they’re offering training to 1.6 million workers instead
item.description: Trillion-dollar retail giant Walmart says it’s not using AI to slash headcounts—instead, it’s providing workers with free access to Google AI training...

item.title: Your Biggest Threat Is Obscurity
item.description: The existence of similar businesses won’t sink you. Being unknown will.

item.title: Just 5% of workers have this skill, according to Google—and they’re 4.5x times more likely to have a higher salary
item.description: The C-suite is pushing for AI productivity gains, but less than half of all workers are using the technology—or getting the proper training, according...

item.title: New Research Says Rural Internet Service Could Be Holding Your Company Back
item.description: In a digital world, even rural employees’ well-being is impacted by internet accessibility and quality.

item.title: Alysa Liu’s Viral Olympic Comeback Proves 1 Thing: Authenticity Sells. So Where Are the Sponsors?
item.description: The figure skater has captured the internet’s heart. Brands should be lining up.

item.title: Figma stock is on the rise again. The software firm just gave a refreshingly human response to a question about AI
item.description: Figma’s fourth-quarter earnings report arrived on Wednesday afternoon with a notable claim from one of its top executives: AI should “complement,” not...

item.title: UPS Is Closing Package Facilities: See the List of Doomed Locations Across Several States in 2026
item.description: After announcing mass layoffs in January, the shipping giant has disclosed in a court filing almost two dozen facilities marked for closure.

item.title: White men file workplace discrimination claims but are less likely to face inequity
item.description: In December 2025, Andrea Lucas, the chair of the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission , invited white men to file more sex- and race-based dis...

item.title: Amazon’s humble history: Jeff Bezos used a rented garage as an office and held team meetings at a local Barnes & Noble
item.description: Amazon's trajectory from a rented garage to a $2.4 trillion enterprise is now business legend.

item.title: AI is running out of power. Space won’t be an escape hatch for decades
item.description: The physics mostly work. The timelines, economics, and infrastructure don’t—at least not yet.

item.title: Royal Caribbean’s CEO is using AI to predict how many hamburgers you’ll eat—and to compete with Orlando, Vegas and Taylor Swift
item.description: "We're massive users of AI in a non-creepy way," Royal Caribbean CEO Jason Liberty told Fortune.

item.title: Why the best problem-solvers think like jazz musicians
item.description: Picture a jazz quartet mid-performance. The bassist anchors the rhythm with meticulous precision—years of practice evident in every note. The saxophon...
